sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Right finger pain.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports pain in the right finger following a skiing injury over the weekend. He describes getting his strap caught on the finger, causing it to bend back. He reports that the pain is exacerbated by movement and has been worsening since the injury. He has been taking ibuprofen for the pain, but it hasn't provided sufficient relief. He also reports experiencing constipation recently and takes miralax for it. He had his appendix removed at age 18 and reports no other surgeries.
Findings
Examination reveals tenderness over the distal phalanx of the index finger, with tenderness over the joint itself. There is pain with flexion. X-rays demonstrate no fractures.
Assessment
The patient has a right hand index finger contusion.
Plan
Order aluminum foam splint and 600 mg ibuprofen every six hours for one week. Schedule follow-up if symptoms do not improve.
